sections.paragraph:
στυγνάζω (στυγνός) 1 aor. ἐστύγνασα

① to be in state of intense dismay, be shocked, appalled (Ezk 26:16 [JZiegler ’52]; 27:35; 28:19; 32:10 ἐπί τινα) so perh. στυγνάσας ἐπὶ τῷ λόγῳ=shocked at the prescription Mk 10:22 (s. 2a below).

② to have a dark or gloomy appearance, be or become gloomy, dark (AcPlTh 35 [Aa 261, 10])

ⓐ of one whose appearance displays sadness or gloom (TestSol 1:4 L; Himerius, Or. [Ecl.] 3, 2; PGM 13, 177; 494; schol. on Aeschyl., Pers. 470; schol. on Soph., Ant. 526; schol. on Apollon. Rhod. 2, 862f; Macrembolites Eustathius 4, 1, 2 Hilberg 1876; Nicetas Eugen. 6, 286 H.) ἐπί τινι at someth., so perh. Mk 10:22 (but s. 1 above).

ⓑ of the appearance of the sky (s. στυγνός: Heraclit. Sto. 39 p. 56, 18) lowering Mt 16:3 (Cat. Cod. Astr. XI/2 p. 179, 19 is dependent on this).—DELG s.v. στυγέω. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

perh. perh. = perhaps

AcPlTh AcPlTh = Acts of Paul and Thecla—List 5

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Himerius Himerius , IV a.d.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Macrembolites Macrembolites , Eustathius, De Hysmenes et Hysmineae Amoribus; XI/XII a.d.—List 5

Nicetas Eugen Nicetas Eugen , XII a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

someth. someth. = something

Heraclit. Sto. Heraclit. Sto. = Heraclitus the Stoic, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 949
